title: Proxmox VLAN 70 Migration
summary: Student validates Proxmox management or service placement on ARIA VLAN 70 as a documentation and verification task unless the instructor explicitly authorizes changes.
student_goal: Confirm the correct VLAN 70 addressing, gateway, bridge, and service reachability after migration without making unapproved management network changes.

required_evidence.label: VLAN 70 IP addressing
required_evidence.description: Student must document expected and actual VLAN 70 IP configuration, including IP, subnet, gateway, and relevant bridge/interface.

required_evidence.label: Gateway reachability
required_evidence.description: Student must confirm the system can reach the VLAN 70 gateway using ping or equivalent evidence.

required_evidence.label: Service reachability
required_evidence.description: Student must confirm ARIA or Proxmox service access after migration using browser, curl, or other approved reachability test.

required_evidence.label: Change boundary
required_evidence.description: Student must state whether this was validation only or whether the instructor authorized any change.

student_workflow: Open the Zammad ticket and identify the system or service being validated on VLAN 70.
Open ARIA AI Mentor and load the assigned ticket.
Collect IP address, gateway, route, bridge/interface, and service reachability evidence.
Treat this as validation/documentation only unless the instructor explicitly authorizes changes.
Do not edit Proxmox management networking, bridges, gateways, or VLAN settings without instructor approval.
opening_questions: What system or service was migrated or validated on VLAN 70?
What IP address, subnet, gateway, and bridge/interface should it use?
Can it reach the VLAN 70 gateway and required services?
Was this validation only, or did the instructor authorize a change?
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not approve migration without gateway and service reachability evidence.
Ask for current IP and bridge evidence before troubleshooting.
Keep rollback considerations visible during network migration work.
Do not recommend Proxmox management network edits without explicit instructor approval.
